item.title: Watch Counterstroke Fiji President Elizabeth Fong on fijivillage Straight Talk
item.description: Watch Counterstroke Fiji President Elizabeth Fong on fijivillage Straight Talk

item.title: Social Welfare e-Transport card recipients to get 50% top-up after bus fare hike
item.description: Social Welfare e-Transport Card users will receive additional assistance from the Government following the recent increase in bus fares. The Ministry of Women, Children and Social Protection says a 50 percent top-up has been provided for beneficiaries under the Transport Assistance Scheme from May to July to help offset the impact of higher transport costs.
item.content:encoded:
Social Welfare e-Transport Card users will receive additional assistance from the Government following the recent increase in bus fares.

The Ministry of Women, Children and Social Protection says a 50 percent top-up has been provided for beneficiaries under the Transport Assistance Scheme from May to July to help offset the impact of higher transport costs.

The Ministry says when Green or Purple e-Transport Cards are used, the full bus fare will still be deducted from the card. However, the Government-funded top-up will provide extra credit to help cushion the effect of the fare increase.

The assistance is expected to benefit eligible Social Welfare recipients who rely on public transportation for their daily travel needs.

Ministry urges people who require further information are being advised to contact their nearest Social Welfare Office.

item.title: FTU calls for 15% pay rise as teachers continue to leave

item.title: Diabetes Fiji calls for dedicated budget allocation in 2026-2027 National Budget
item.description: Diabetes Fiji is calling on the government to establish a dedicated budget allocation for Diabetes Fiji in the 2026-2027 National

item.title: HART calls for relief measures as rising costs put pressure on vulnerable families
item.description: HART CEO Paserio Furivai says families in HART communities are facing increasing hardship as rising food and fuel prices put pressure on already limited household budgets. Furivai says HART has invested in education through kindergartens, primary schools and tutoring programmes, but many families are unable to prioritise education because their immediate focus is meeting basic needs and putting fo
item.content:encoded: HART CEO Paserio Furivai says families in HART communities are facing increasing hardship as rising food and fuel prices put pressure on already limited household budgets. Furivai says HART has invested in education through kindergartens, primary schools and tutoring programmes, but many families are unable to prioritise education because their immediate focus is meeting basic needs and putting food on the table. He says many families rely on welfare assistance and odd jobs to survive, and with the rising cost of living, they are being forced to make sacrifices as their money buys fewer essential items.

item.title: ‘Beauty and the Beast’ and ‘A Whole New World’ singer, Peabo Bryson dies at 75
item.description: Peabo Bryson best known for for singing soul ballads, and particularly duets has died at the age of 75. The singer was known for 1

item.title: Veronica and Manoa Malani submit application to register ‘Liberation of Fiji Party’
item.description: The Registrar of Political Parties, Ana Mataiciwa has received the application for registration of the proposed ‘Liberation of Fiji Party’. Members of the proposed party include Veronica and Manoa Malani.
item.content:encoded:
The Registrar of Political Parties, Ana Mataiciwa has received the application for registration of the proposed ‘Liberation of Fiji Party’.

Members of the proposed party include Veronica and Manoa Malani.

Mataiciwa confirms that the Fijian Elections Office is now preparing to publish the party’s application in a newspaper and in the Gazette, as required under the Political Parties (Registration, Conduct, Funding and Disclosures) Act 2013.

Mataiciwa says once the publication is made, the objections period will officially open.

She says any person may submit a written objection to the FEO regarding the party’s application for registration, during the objections period.

The Registrar says they will assess and respond to all objections within 7 days of receipt, after giving the proposed party an opportunity to respond, and afterwards inform both the objectors and the applicant party of the decision.

Mataiciwa says the proposed political party will be advised on whether its application for registration has been approved or rejected, after the completion of this process.

She reiterated that this decision will be made after a thorough compliance check with all requirements set out in the Political Parties (Registration, Conduct, Funding and Disclosures) Act 2013.

The FEO stresses the importance of transparency and public participation in the Political Party Registration Process.

It ensures that all proposed parties meet the legal requirements and that the public has the opportunity to engage meaningfully.

item.title: 60 students affected as RSL transport fails to turn up for 2nd day
item.description: About 60 students remain affected after a Rural Service Licence transport operator failed to turn up for a second consecutive day
